data.title: J&J CAR-T therapy for B-cell lymphoma shows promise in phase 1
data.description:
• Phase 1b data on Johnson and Johnson's experimental CAR T-cell therapy JNJ-4496 found a high complete response rate at the recommended phase 2 dose in patients with large B-cell lymphoma
  • Among evaluable patients at that dose, a 75%-80% complete response rate was observed.
  • Patients enrolled in the study had not been previously treated with a CAR-T therapy.
  • JNJ-4496 targets CD19 and CD20 antigens, two cell surface proteins frequently found on malignant B-cells.

data.title: OpenAI employees sold $240M in last funding round, bringing total to $3B: report
data.description:
• Employees of generative artificial intelligence maker OpenAI sold $240M worth of shares in the company's last funding round, The Information reported.
  • That brings the total sold by employees to $3B since 2021, the news outlet added. For comparison purposes, SpaceX (SPACE) employees have sold a similar amount, The Information added, citing data from private markets company Caplight.
  • SoftBank (OTCPK:SFTBY), which led the ChatGPT maker's most recent funding round in April, has acquired nearly half of the current and former employee shares. In total, SoftBank has acquired $1.5B worth of OpenAI shares from current and former employees.
  • OpenAI, which became the world's most valuable startup after the April $40B founding round, is backed by Microsoft (NASDAQ:MSFT) after a “multibillion” dollar investment in January 2023.
  • OpenAI and SoftBank did not immediately respond to a request for comment.

data.title: U.S. stocks slump as Israel says Iran has fired 'projectiles'
data.description:
Wall Street's major averages on Friday moved back towards their session lows, after Israel's military said Iran had fired projectiles against the country. The move came after Israel late on Thursday attacked some of Iran's nuclear sites and military targets, raising fears of an all-out war in the Middle East.

The benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) fell as much as -1.2%, and was last -1.0% in late afternoon trade. The Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was also -1.0%, and the Dow (DJI) was -1.8%.

"All of Israel is under fire as Iran fires projectiles," the Israel Defense Forces said on X (formerly Twitter).   

On Thursday, Israel hit Iran with a series of what it called "preemptive" strikes. Iran retaliated by launching over 100 drones at Israel overnight. Israeli airspace has been closed, and a state of emergency has been declared amid expectations of another counterattack.

WTI crude oil futures (CL1:COM) and Brent crude futures (CO1:COM) - the international oil benchmark - surged in response, last +7.3% and +7.1%, respectively. Energy stocks rose in tandem, and the sector was easily the top gainer on Friday.  

“In economic and financial terms, Israel's attack on Iran constitutes a negative shock at an already fragile time. With growth and inflation concerns already high, key policy tools limited, and the global architecture shaken, the adverse effects are likely to be both direct and indirect,” said Mohamed El-Erian, part-time chief economic advisor at Allianz, on X (formerly Twitter).

“The severity of the impact will depend on the magnitude and duration of the attack, as well as the retaliation it triggers,” El-Erian said.

Over in the bond market, yields were higher. The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) was up 7 basis points to 4.44%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) rose 6 basis points to 3.97%.

Friday's economic calendar was light. The consumer sentiment index rose to 60.5 in June, its first improvement in six months, from 52.2 in May, and exceeding the 53.5 consensus, according to data released by the University of Michigan.

“Usually, such a set of favorable UMich consumer sentiment numbers would attract quite a bit of attention,” El-Erian added. “The worry, however, is that the positive these numbers suggest may be overwhelmed by the surge in oil prices and worsening economic uncertainty resulting from the latest developments in the Middle East.”

Turning to active movers, Photoshop maker Adobe (ADBE) was -5% despite reporting a beat-and-raise quarter.

data.title: Israel-Iran tensions hit travel stocks, airlines cancel flights over region - update
data.description:
Updated 14:05ET with carriers that have canceled flights to and from Israel.

Heightened geopolitical tensions fueled by Israel's airstrike on Iran are weighing on travel-related stocks, with airline, cruise, and hotel shares all trading in the red.

Airline stocks are bearing the brunt of the selloff, particularly carriers with significant exposure to international routes. Shares of United Airlines (NASDAQ:UAL), Delta Air Lines (NYSE:DAL), and American Airlines Group (NASDAQ:AAL) are underperforming the sector, each down 4% to 5%, while the U.S. Global Jets ETF (NYSEARCA:JETS) is off 3%.

Cruise stocks are also in the red led by a ~5% drop in shares of Carnival Corporation (NYSE:CCL) whose Cunard line operates itineraries in the Mediterranean and Western Africa. Royal Caribbean (NYSE:RCL), Norwegian Cruise Lines (NYSE:NCLH), and Viking Holdings (NYSE:VIK) are also trading defensively.

Losses have spilled into hotel stocks, as well as online travel platforms including Expedia (NASDAQ:EXPE), Booking Holdings (NASDAQ:BKNG), Tripadvisor (NASDAQ:TRIP), Airbnb (NASDAQ:ABNB), and Travelzoo (NASDAQ:TZOO).

With the airspace closed over Israel, Iran, Iraq, Jordan, and Syria, major carriers are suspending flights to Israel, with some turning back mid-flight at the start of hostilities between Israel and Iran. Among those canceling flights until September include Delta (NYSE:DAL), United (NASDAQ:UAL) and American Airlines (NASDAQ:AAL). Others that have suspended service to and from Israel include Air France, Israel's El Al, Turkish Airlines and Wizz Air. Lufthansa has suspended flights between Tel Aviv and Tehran until July 31, while Emirates is canceling flights from Dubai to Iran, Iraq, Jordan, and Lebanon.  

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: NHTSA to streamline ADS exemption in time for Tesla's Cybercab launch
data.description:
Under the Trump Administration, the National Highway Traffic Safety Administration is making it easier and quicker for automakers to deploy self-driving cars by “streamlining” the automotive driving systems (“ADS”) exemption process necessary for vehicles without a steering wheel or brake pedals.

Under the current standards mandated by the National Highway Traffic Safety Administration, any full self-driving vehicle for commercial use without driver controls must obtain an exemption under “Part 555” of the National Traffic and Motor Vehicle Safety Act. As part of its new mandate, NHTSA is taking a “dynamic and flexible approach to evaluate these exemptions” to allow the agency to process ADS and other exemptions “within months, rather than years.”

“NHTSA must improve its Part 555 processing times substantially to keep pace with the rapid innovation of the ADS industry and to ensure that exemptions remain effective tools for nurturing groundbreaking safety technologies,” the agency said in a notice on its website.

The news comes just in time for Tesla’s (NASDAQ:TSLA) Cybercab launch in Austin, Texas on June 22 and bolstered shares in Friday’s afternoon trading. Faced with a lengthy regulatory process, Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA), and others including Alphabet’s (GOOG) Waymo, would have to potentially wait years to comply with NHTSA regulations.

data.title: Applied Digital falls after CoreWeave sells stake
data.description:
Shares of Applied Digital (NASDAQ:APLD) fell about 8% on Friday after a regulatory filing showed that CoreWeave (NASDAQ:CRWV) sold its entire stake in the company.

CoreWeave's stock rose about 3% on Friday.

A Schedule 13G filing showed that CoreWeave now beneficially owns zero shares of Applied Digital’s common stock, 0.0% of the class.

Earlier this month, it was reported that Applied Digital secured two, 15-year lease agreements with AI hyperscaler CoreWeave which is expected to generate $7B in total revenue over the lifetime of the deals.

An 8-K filing (from June 2) said that on May 28, in connection with the entry into the Datacenter Leases, Applied Digital issued to CoreWeave a warrant to acquire up to 13,062,521 common shares at an exercise price of $7.19 per share.

data.title: Tanker stocks rally as Israel airstrikes against Iran lift freight rates
data.description:
Tanker stocks jumped Friday after Israel’s overnight airstrikes on Iran drove up freight rates, with one of the world's top tanker operators saying it is more wary of leasing out its vessels in the region.

Forward freight agreements for July surged as much as 15% to $12.83/metric ton, according to data from the Marex brokerage, before paring gains to ~12%, and benchmark tanker rates from the region soared, with the cost of hiring vessels to move Saudi Arabian oil to China soaring up to 24% to 53.85 Worldscale points, according to the Baltic Exchange in London.

Among potentially relevant stocks: Frontline (NYSE:FRO) +5.3%, Dorian LPG (NYSE:LPG) +5.1%, Tsakos Energy Navigation (TEN) +3.8%, Nordic American Tankers (NAT) +3.3%, DHT Holdings (DHT) +2.8%, International Seaways (INSW) +2.5%, Teekay (TK) +2.5%, Torm (TRMD) +2.4%, Hafnia (HAFN) +2.3%, ZIM Integrated Shipping (ZIM) +2.2%, BW LPG (BWLP) +2.1%, Navios Maritime Partners (BMM) +1.8%, Scorpio Tankers (STNG) +1%, Danaos (DAC) +0.9%, Ardmore Shipping (ASC) +0.5%.

Iran has not yet threatened to close the critical Strait of Hormuz to traffic in retaliation for the attacks, although analysts generally believe any retaliation more likely would occur in the Red Sea.

"The Strait of Hormuz remains open and commercial traffic continues to flow uninterrupted," the U.S.-led Combined Maritime Force said in an advisory, but the airstrikes have raised the likelihood of regional conflict to "significant."

Ship owners reportedly are exercising extra caution and opting to stay away from the Red Sea and the Persian Gulf, the BIMCO shipping association said, adding that if the U.S. is perceived to be involved in any attacks, "the risk of escalation increases significantly."

Fearnley shipbrokers global head of research Dag Kilen said hopes for normalization of vessel transits through the Red Sea have been dashed in the wake of Israel's attack on Iran.

Swedish bank SEB said the attacks are a short-term positive for tankers, but long-term impacts likely are more negative if higher oil prices result in demand disruptions.

"We're far more hesitant" about offering vessels to haul cargoes from the Persian Gulf, Frontline (NYSE:FRO) CEO Lars Barstad told Bloomberg, adding that he is not yet aware of any major changes in how owners are operating their vessels, although the company is being extra vigilant with tankers in the area.

Tanker owners will take their cue from insurers in determining the risks associated with the trade, Barstad said, but it is too soon after the attacks for such a calculation.

data.title: NBA and Take-Two expand partnership, unveil new media company
data.description:
The National Basketball Association will continue its partnership with game publisher Take-Two (NASDAQ:TTWO) under a new multiyear agreement.

As part of the partnership, the company will continue to develop the NBA 2K game series, according to a joint statement on Friday.

Additionally, the parties are launching a new company, NBA Take-Two Media, that will produce stories and experiences across gaming, travel, music, fashion, and food, among other things, in partnership with tastemakers, athletes, content creators, and fans.

“NBA 2K has engaged a new generation of basketball fans in an increasingly digital world,” NBA Commissioner Adam Silver said. “By expanding our partnership and creating NBA Take-Two Media, we look forward to building on the incredible success of the NBA 2K franchise.”

"We are excited to unveil NBA Take-Two Media and look forward to seeing how it will create an entirely new and multifaceted NBA experience for our communities,” Take-Two CEO Strauss Zelnick said.

The league said 2K has also agreed to extend its relationships with the WNBA, NBA G League, and USA Basketball.

data.title: Wall Financial GAAP EPS of C$0.18, revenue of C$43.79M
data.description:
• Wall Financial press release (OTCPK:WFICF): Q1 GAAP EPS of C$0.18.
  • Revenue of C$43.79M (+21.0% Y/Y).

data.title: RH 'moving in the right direction' -- Morgan Stanley
data.description:
Having cycled through the low point of the post-Liberation Day headwinds, RH (NYSE:RH) is moving in the right direction and is free cash flow positive for the first time in more than a year, said Morgan Stanley’s Simeon Gutman, reflecting Wall Street’s enthusiasm towards the home furnishings company with a 25% rally in the stock from Thursday’s close.

After the close on Thursday, RH (NYSE:RH) swung to a profit with a 12% increase in sales, beating expectations despite operating in “the worst housing market in 50 years.” The company also said it would continue to take mitigation measures to offset tariffs to include a reduction in China sourcing to just 2% and making vendors absorb a “meaningful portion” of tariff-related costs, all of which launched shares higher in after-hours trading.

The company is not completely out of the woods, however, with $200M to $300M in excess inventory likely to pressure margins, high financial leverage, and lingering macro headwinds along with a stubbornly stagnant housing market weighing on demand. But with new collections luring in customers (and underpinning margins) and the ramp-up of its European galleries, RH (NYSE:RH) revenue growth should top 11% in FY25 with its adjusted operating margin up ~3 pts to 14.2%, Gutman predicts.

Friedman concedes that higher prices might be necessary to offset the deeper membership discount and additional promotions on its outdoor furniture. But with a more desired brand comes more price flexibility.

“People will pay more for better things,” Friedman said, adding that the company is selling many fewer categories, much higher quality product at higher prices.

“We have worked hard to destroy the former version of ourselves and are in the process of unleashing what we believe is an exponentially more inspiring and disruptive RH brand,” CEO Gary Friedman added on the company’s earnings call.

Wall Street analysts and Seeking Alpha authors share a Buy consensus on RH (RH), while Seeking Alpha’s Quant rating is more cautious, recently upgrading the stock to Hold from Sell with a Quant score of 2.61 out of 5.
